query_normalized: linking resting-state fmri entropy to real-world decision risk‑taking neuroscience
rationale: The paper provides a methodological approach to quantify the complexity of resting‑state BOLD signals (via fractal behavior and Hurst exponents), which is a same‑mechanism measure related to rs‑fMRI entropy (acceptance category a) and can serve as a foundational reference for complexity metrics in resting‑state studies.
